behavior: Omnivorous, bold and inquisitive; commonly forages on the ground alone or in pairs, and flies with slow wingbeats, glides, and soaring.
field_marks: Very large, thick-necked corvid with a heavy, straight bill
Entirely black plumage, including bill, eyes, and legs
Long, wedge- or diamond-shaped tail in flight
Long, narrow wings with distinct fingered wingtips
habitat: Highly adaptable across open and forested landscapes, including woodland, cliffs and coasts, mountains, tundra, grassland, scrub, desert, and human-modified areas.
